VideoTitle: Dynamic Programming - Learn to Solve Algorithmic Problems & Coding Challenges
CommentText:
Definitely, one of the best courses to start with DP. Just started with the tabulation approach and, decided to attempt it after watching the initial explanation. But decided to follow a different approach as below:
def fibDPTabulation(position):
    if position <= 1: return 1
    table = list()
    table = [0 for i in range(0, position+1)]
    table[1] = 1
    
    length = len(table)
    for i in range(2, length):
        table[i] = table[i-2] + table[i-1]
    
    return table[position]
The approach uses the same logic as the basic looping approach but uses an extra list/array for the purpose of keeping track of the calculations.

The brutal bedside manners (or lack thereof) of doctors, the complete disregard for human dignity, privacy, etc. These things are BUILT INTO those systems, and actually many of those countries' citizens think it's pretty normal and they may even be ok with it, but I would seriously caution americans to not assume that what they don't know about is actually what they want. VideoTitle: Programming Portfolio - Everything that you want to know
CommentText:
To add about self-hosting. I've played around with self-hosting for a long time. I literally just jumped into it with absolutely no idea what I was doing which I honestly think is the best way to learn. Domain names are paid yearly, I highly recommend a legitimate one like Namecheap. Many of the others like GoDaddy are legitimate and legal but very shady and sneaky. You can get one for like $3 or $5 for the entire year, just that one $5 flat fee. You don't need anything expensive.


For a server the best bet is just a completely blank remote computer. Digital Ocean provides this, their not-shady, their completely legitimate, and their cheapest computer is $5 a month. They have a wonderful pricing model with no hidden costs, fees, etc... that many companies hide away in fine print. The downside is your going to start working with nothing, you have a blank Linux computer with nothing on it, not even a webserver. So, yes, you'll have to set that up yourself. I'd even sugest getting a friend to help you if you don't want to jump in. But It's $5 a month, with a great company, great pricing model, and you have total control of the server so it's worth it.


Anyways, if your interested in the self-hosting option ~_^

VideoTitle: #32 Python Tutorial for Beginners | Functions in Python
CommentText:
A common problem in user interface involves imposing a stable order upon elements displayed upon a page. While the initial order is easy to specify, keeping it consistent with user actions is interesting.

Take for example a menu structure that looks something like this :

Apple
  Sour
  Green
  Red
Banana
  Ripe
  Raw
  Fried
    Spicy
    Sweet
    Salted
Cats
  Large
    Black
    White
  Small
    Grey
    Striped
                            
Now imagine an operation on this structure that drag-drops an element from any position to any position in the tree.

For example, moveBelow(Apple/Red, Banana/Fried/Spicy).

The (strange) new tree after this operation looks as shown below. The Red element is now sitting under Banana/Fried/Spicy as specified.

Apple
  Sour
  Green
Banana
  Ripe
  Raw
  Fried
    Spicy
    Red
    Sweet
    Salted
Cats
  Large
    Black
    White
  Small
    Grey
    Striped
                            
Note that it is possible to move not just leaf elements but also subtree, as in moveBelow (Banana/Fried, Cats/Small). This yields an even stranger tree as shown below. The entire Fried submenu is now placed after Cats/Small, on the path Cats/Fried.

Apple
  Sour
  Green
Banana
  Ripe
  Raw
Cats
  Large
    Black
    White
  Small
    Grey
    Striped
  Fried
    Spicy
    Red
    Sweet
    Salted

                              
Implement a data structure to represent the menu, and the function moveBelow (fromPath, belowPath)

Use any programming language you like. The function should operate upon the data structure and leave it in a correct state after it is done; it should be possible to see the order (and path!) of each element in the datastructure.

CommentText:
For anyone Facing difficulties like me , while using laravel 8 version ....
I did this .
first reset everything.

rm -rf node_modules
npm cache clear --force


i cleared my package.json file , as it is not having many dependecies mentioned in this video.
Then go to github link provided by Brad in description, copy package.json file from there.
Next , follow shiks Tv comment.
@Shiks TV


1. Make Sure in /resources/js/app.js there is a code : require('./bootstrap');
2. run in your terminal -> composer require laravel/ui
3 run in your terminal -> php artisan ui bootstrap


Next , 
 npm install
npm run dev

This gave me some errors regarding bootstrap and Saas loader.

 npm uninstall --save-dev sass-loader
 npm install --save-dev sass-loader@7.1.0

I rectified it using above commands.
Now use,
 npm install
npm run dev

You might get some warnings , no worries  !!! it would work.
